2. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is December 17, 2024.
a. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise.
b. Before a party files and serves a summary judgment/adjudication motion, it must contact the clerk to make a reservation.
c. The Court allows a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared, unless good cause is found to exceed this number. Contact the clerk to schedule a good cause hearing.

7. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than October 3, 2024.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-21-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, not contested, and the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendant Clausen-Patten, Inc.'s Motion for Summary Judgment or, in the Alternative, Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Opposition filed by Plaintiffs. Reply filed by Defendant.

1. Defendant fails to sustain its initial burden of showing Plaintiffs do not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, needed evidence of asbestos exposure and causation. (See Aguilar v. Atl. Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 855.) Defendant enclosed excerpts of Volume 5 of the Deposition of Plaintiff Joseph Fare, taken on November 27, 2023, in its moving evidence. (See Hollingsworth Decl. at 2:16-17 [par. 6].) Defendant also enclosed Plaintiffs' Answers to Standard Interrogatories. (See Hollingsworth Decl. at 2:14-15 [par. 5].)

a. With respect to Joseph Fare's deposition, McGonnell is distinguishable. There, the plaintiff provided an affirmative response in his deposition regarding his recall of certain products. (See McGonnell v. Kaiser Gypsum Co. (2002) 98 Cal.App.4th 1098, 1104; see Weber v. John Crane, Inc. (2006) 143 Cal.App.4th 1433, 1440 [summarizing same].) Here, Joseph Fare provided negative responses (see Hollingsworth Decl., Ex. D [Depo. of Joseph Fare, Dated Nov. 27, 2023] at 3:20-4:24) and a speculative response (see id. at 4:25-5:11), which is analogous to Weber. Joseph Fare's deposition does "not create an inference either of nonexposure or of the inability to prove exposure by some other means." (Weber, supra, 143 Cal.App.4th at p. 1439.) All that can be extrapolated is that Joseph Fare himself (not others) lacks personal knowledge of asbestos exposure. Defendant fails to "show that, after extensive discovery, [P]laintiffs asserted they had no additional information" as was the case with the deposition in McGonnell. (Id. at p. 1442.)

b. With respect to Plaintiffs' responses to Standard Interrogatories, Weber is instructive. Defendant fails to "support its motion with evidence that [P]laintiffs failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive interrogatories designed to elicit all the evidence [P]laintiffs had to support their contention of liability" as was the case with the special interrogatory responses in Andrews v. Foster Wheeler LLC (2006) 138 Cal.App.4th 96. (Weber, supra, 143 Cal.App.4th at p. 1442 [emphasis added as to comprehensive].) "A motion for summary judgment is not a mechanism for rewarding limited discovery[.]" (Ibid.)

2. Defendant's motion for summary adjudication of Plaintiff Elaine Fare's loss of consortium cause of action is denied for the same reasons.

The prevailing party must prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and submit it to Department 301.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Not Present. Not Reported. (301/RCE)

MINI MINUTES: (PART 2 OF 2)
(Mini Minutes continued from previous entry)

The trial date is February 26, 2024 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day under C.C.P. section 36(f) is March 8, 2024. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is February 22, 2024. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone.

Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is February 9, 2024. Expert discovery cut-off date is February 23, 2024. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than December 8, 2023. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
No later than November 23, 2023, plaintiff must provide a statement to each defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims as to each defendant. No later than November 23, 2023, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown.

A further case management conference is scheduled for January 11, 2024 at 1:30 p.m. There is no need for any party to file a case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two days prior to take the CMC off calendar. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than January 12, 2024. Responses to the demand are due no later than January 26, 2024. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands/offers stating their best and final settlement effort. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Pang Ly via electronic mail by February 16, 2024 at 12:00 p.m. Mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, February 22, 2024 at 9:00 a.m. in 603. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is granted excuse based on good cause. Court to issue and serve order. Judge: Samuel K. Feng, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Cheri Bullock of CHERIBULLOCKCSR INC., CSR #4714, CheriBullock@gmail.com (Tel: 661-645-4048). (503/SKF)
